review::.::holistic::high_level_elegance.summary: TerminalFrame interface defined in data/terminal.ts instead of centralized types/index.ts

evidence: src/lib/types/index.ts exports Artefact, Project, ProjectGitHubData, and SectionAccent — establishing a clear convention that domain types live here
src/lib/data/artefacts.ts imports its Artefact type from '$lib/types', following the convention
src/lib/data/projects.ts imports its Project type from '$lib/types', following the convention
src/lib/data/terminal.ts defines TerminalFrame locally on line 1 and exports it from the data file, breaking the established type-ownership pattern
detail.suggestion: Move the TerminalFrame interface to src/lib/types/index.ts and import it in terminal.ts, matching the pattern used by artefacts.ts and projects.ts.

review::.::holistic::convention_outlier:.summary: terminal.ts defines TerminalFrame inline while sibling data modules import types from $lib/types

evidence: src/lib/data/artefacts.ts imports 'type { Artefact } from $lib/types' (line 1)
src/lib/data/projects.ts imports 'type { Project } from $lib/types' (line 1)
src/lib/data/terminal.ts defines 'export interface TerminalFrame' inline (lines 1-4) instead of importing from the shared types module
The holistic_context sibling_behavior scan confirms terminal.ts is missing the 'type' pattern that 2/3 siblings share
detail.suggestion: Move the TerminalFrame interface to src/lib/types/index.ts alongside Project, Artefact, and SectionAccent. Then change terminal.ts to import it: import type { TerminalFrame } from '$lib/types';

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.summary: All catch blocks in github.ts discard error context, making network failures invisible during builds

evidence: fetchJson (line 12): catch block returns null with no logging or error wrapping, discarding HTTP status, rate-limit headers, and exception details
fetchRepoData commit-count block (line 52): catch swallows with only a comment, silently producing commitCount: 0 for any failure
Three separate catch blocks, all following the same silent-swallow pattern
detail.suggestion: Add a minimal console.warn in each catch block that includes the URL and error message (e.g., console.warn(`GitHub fetch failed for ${url}:`, error)). This preserves the graceful-degradation behavior while giving developers build-time visibility into API failures, rate limiting, or network issues.

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.summary: Outer try-catch in +page.server.ts is redundant since fetchAllRepoData already handles all errors internally

evidence: +page.server.ts line 15-19: wraps fetchAllRepoData in try-catch, but fetchAllRepoData delegates to fetchRepoData which delegates to fetchJson, and all failures are already caught and converted to null at the inner layers
The outer catch can only fire if Promise.all itself throws (an engine-level failure) or if the .map/.filter on project arrays throws, neither of which is a network error
detail.suggestion: Either remove the outer try-catch (since the callee already guarantees no throws for network failures) or, if defensive coding at the SvelteKit load boundary is intentional, add a comment explaining that this guards against unexpected non-network failures rather than duplicating the network error handling.

review::.::holistic::naming_quality::wit.summary: withGithub does not communicate that it merges static project data with fetched GitHub metadata

evidence: The function 'withGithub' on line 22 of +page.svelte takes projects without GitHub data and enriches them by looking up their repo in the fetched githubData record. The name 'withGithub' reads like a predicate or filter rather than an enrichment/merge operation.
detail.suggestion: Rename to 'enrichWithGithubData' or 'mergeGithubData' to communicate the enrichment operation: function enrichWithGithubData(projects: Omit<Project, 'github'>[]): Project[]

review::.::holistic::mid_level_elegance:.summary: accentColors record duplicated in Section.svelte and ProjectCard.svelte with divergent 'none' values

evidence: Section.svelte defines accentColors with none: 'transparent', while ProjectCard.svelte defines the same mapping with none: 'var(--border)'. Both components independently translate SectionAccent to CSS values, meaning this boundary concept is resolved in two places with inconsistent results.
detail.suggestion: Extract the accentColors mapping to a shared location (e.g., a small utility in $lib/utils/accent.ts or alongside the SectionAccent type in $lib/types/index.ts). If the 'none' value intentionally differs between contexts, make that explicit with two named exports (e.g., sectionAccentColors and cardAccentColors) so the divergence is visible and intentional rather than accidental.

review::.::holistic::mid_level_elegance:.summary: Terminal.svelte imports TerminalFrame from data module instead of types barrel, unlike all other components

evidence: Terminal.svelte imports TerminalFrame from '$lib/data/terminal', while ProjectCard.svelte, Artefacts.svelte, and Section.svelte all import their types from '$lib/types'. The TerminalFrame interface is defined directly in terminal.ts rather than in the types barrel, creating an import path inconsistency at the data-component boundary.
detail.suggestion: Move the TerminalFrame interface to $lib/types/index.ts alongside Project, Artefact, and SectionAccent. Update terminal.ts to import and use it from there, and update Terminal.svelte to import from '$lib/types'. This makes the type boundary consistent: all component prop types come from $lib/types, all data modules export typed values.

review::.::holistic::package_organizatio.summary: TerminalFrame interface defined in data/terminal.ts instead of centralized types/index.ts

evidence: src/lib/types/index.ts centralizes all other shared types (Project, ProjectGitHubData, Artefact, SectionAccent), but TerminalFrame is defined inline at src/lib/data/terminal.ts:1-4
artefacts.ts and projects.ts both import their types from '$lib/types', establishing a convention that terminal.ts breaks
detail.suggestion: Move the TerminalFrame interface to src/lib/types/index.ts alongside the other type definitions, then import it in terminal.ts. Staged plan: (1) Add 'export interface TerminalFrame { text: string; delay: number; }' to src/lib/types/index.ts. (2) Replace the inline interface in src/lib/data/terminal.ts with 'import type { TerminalFrame } from "$lib/types";'. (3) Update any component that may import TerminalFrame from terminal.ts to import from $lib/types instead. (4) Run 'bun run build' to verify no import errors.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::f.summary: fetchRepoData bypasses its own fetchJson helper for the commit-count request

evidence: Lines 31-35 use fetchJson<T> for repo, languages, and contributors calls, but lines 42-53 use raw fetch() with manually duplicated Accept header for commit count
The raw fetch block also duplicates the try/catch-return-null pattern that fetchJson already encapsulates
detail.suggestion: Extract the commit-count fetch into a small helper (e.g., fetchCommitCount) that uses fetchJson or at minimum reuses the same header constant. The Link-header parsing is the genuinely distinct logic; the fetch setup and error handling should not be repeated.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::p.summary: Three identically-typed project arrays force repetitive spreading in every consumer

evidence: +page.server.ts lines 7-10 manually spreads all three arrays to collect repo names: [...impactProjects.map(p => p.repo), ...explorationProjects.map(p => p.repo), ...metaProjects.map(p => p.repo)]
+page.svelte imports all three arrays separately and calls withGithub() on each independently (lines 114, 131, 142)
All three arrays share the exact same type: Omit<Project, 'github'>[]
detail.suggestion: Define a single allProjects array or record keyed by category ('impact' | 'exploration' | 'meta'). Consumers can then iterate or filter by category, and adding a new category requires only a data change, not updates to every consumer.

review::.::holistic::logic_clarity::redu.summary: Redundant `if (browser)` guard inside onMount which only runs in the browser

evidence: Line 20: `if (browser) {` inside onMount callback. SvelteKit's onMount is guaranteed to run only in the browser, making this check always true and the import of `browser` from `$app/environment` unnecessary for this purpose.
detail.suggestion: Remove the `if (browser)` wrapper and the `browser` import. Assign `prefersReducedMotion` directly: `prefersReducedMotion = window.matchMedia('(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)').matches;`

review::.::holistic::logic_clarity::alwa.summary: `.filter((repo) => repo.length > 0)` is always true; all repo strings in the data are non-empty

evidence: Line 11 of +page.server.ts filters repos by length > 0, but every project in impactProjects, explorationProjects, and metaProjects has a non-empty `repo` string literal. The filter never removes any element.
detail.suggestion: Remove the `.filter((repo) => repo.length > 0)` call. If the intent is defensive (guarding against future data with empty repos), add a comment explaining the guard, e.g., `// Skip entries without a repo path`.

review::.::holistic::type_safety::unsafe.summary: fetchJson uses unsafe 'as T' cast on unvalidated API response JSON

evidence: Line 11: 'return (await res.json()) as T' casts the parsed JSON to generic type T without any runtime validation. RepoResponse, Record<string, number>, and ContributorResponse[] are all asserted but never checked against actual API response shape.
detail.suggestion: Add a lightweight runtime shape check (e.g., using Zod or a manual guard function) before the cast, or at minimum validate that expected fields exist. For example, for RepoResponse, check that 'stargazers_count' and 'updated_at' are present and of correct types before returning.

review::.::holistic::type_safety::termin.summary: TerminalFrame interface defined in data file instead of canonical types directory

evidence: TerminalFrame is defined and exported from src/lib/data/terminal.ts (lines 1-4), while all other shared interfaces (Project, ProjectGitHubData, Artefact, SectionAccent) are defined in src/lib/types/index.ts. The Terminal.svelte component imports the type from the data file: import type { TerminalFrame } from '$lib/data/terminal'.
detail.suggestion: Move the TerminalFrame interface to src/lib/types/index.ts alongside the other shared types, and update imports in both terminal.ts and Terminal.svelte to import from '$lib/types'.

review::.::holistic::type_safety::commit.summary: commitCount typed as number uses 0 for both 'zero commits' and 'unknown count'

evidence: In github.ts lines 40-53, commitCount is initialized to 0 and stays at 0 if the link header is missing, the regex doesn't match, or the fetch throws. The type ProjectGitHubData.commitCount is 'number', making 0 indistinguishable from 'could not determine'. A consumer displaying commit count cannot differentiate between a repo with no commits and a failed count retrieval.
detail.suggestion: Type commitCount as 'number | null' in ProjectGitHubData, use null to represent 'unknown', and update consumers to handle the null case (e.g., show 'N/A' instead of '0').

review::.::holistic::contract_coherence:.summary: TerminalFrame interface defined in data file instead of types module, breaking module-level API convention

evidence: All other shared types (Project, ProjectGitHubData, Artefact, SectionAccent) are exported from src/lib/types/index.ts
TerminalFrame is defined and exported from src/lib/data/terminal.ts (line 1-4)
Terminal.svelte imports TerminalFrame from '$lib/data/terminal' rather than '$lib/types', meaning a component must import from a data module to get a type
detail.suggestion: Move the TerminalFrame interface to src/lib/types/index.ts and re-export it. Update the import in src/lib/data/terminal.ts and src/lib/components/Terminal.svelte to use '$lib/types'.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: accentColors record duplicated in Section.svelte and ProjectCard.svelte with divergent 'none' values

evidence: Section.svelte defines accentColors with none: 'transparent' (line 13-18), while ProjectCard.svelte defines it with none: 'var(--border)' (line 13-18). Both are Record<SectionAccent, string> mapping the same SectionAccent type to CSS values.
detail.suggestion: Extract a shared accentColors mapping (or a getAccentColor utility) into a shared location (e.g. a small helper alongside the SectionAccent type). If the 'none' value intentionally differs per context, make the divergence explicit through a parameter rather than silent duplication.

review::.::holistic::low_level_elegance:.summary: $effect in +page.svelte combines two unrelated observer responsibilities in a single 50-line block

evidence: Lines 40-92 of +page.svelte contain a single $effect that sets up both a reveal animation IntersectionObserver and a scroll-driven background tint IntersectionObserver, with a combined cleanup function
detail.suggestion: Extract two separate functions (e.g., setupRevealObserver and setupTintObserver) and call both from the $effect, or use two separate $effect blocks. Each observer has independent lifecycle and purpose.

review::.::holistic::low_level_elegance:.summary: Commit count fetch in fetchRepoData bypasses the module's own fetchJson helper, duplicating fetch setup

evidence: Lines 41-54: fetchRepoData manually calls fetch() with the same Accept header that fetchJson already encapsulates, but needs access to response headers. This creates two fetch patterns in the same module.
detail.suggestion: Extract a lower-level helper (e.g., fetchResponse) that returns the Response object, then build fetchJson on top of it. Use fetchResponse for the commit-count call where header access is needed. This keeps one fetch configuration point.

review::.::holistic::low_level_elegance:.summary: fetchAllRepoData uses forEach with index to correlate names to results instead of a more direct construction

evidence: Lines 72-76: unique.forEach((name, i) => { data[name] = results[i]; }) relies on positional correlation between the unique array and Promise.all results, which is correct but indirect
detail.suggestion: Replace with Object.fromEntries(unique.map((name, i) => [name, results[i]])) or a reduce, which makes the name-to-result mapping declarative rather than imperative.

review::.::holistic::cross_module_archit.summary: TerminalFrame interface defined in data module instead of the shared types module

evidence: src/lib/data/terminal.ts defines and exports 'TerminalFrame' interface at line 1, while all other domain types (Project, ProjectGitHubData, Artefact, SectionAccent) live in src/lib/types/index.ts

src/lib/components/Terminal.svelte imports TerminalFrame from '$lib/data/terminal' (line 2), unlike sibling components (Artefacts.svelte, ProjectCard.svelte, Section.svelte) which all import types from '$lib/types'
detail.suggestion: Move the TerminalFrame interface to src/lib/types/index.ts alongside the other domain types, and update the imports in both terminal.ts and Terminal.svelte to use '$lib/types'. This aligns with the established pattern where all domain interfaces live in the types module.

review::.::holistic::test_strategy::zero.summary: No test framework, test files, or test scripts exist anywhere in the codebase

evidence: package.json contains no test-related devDependencies (vitest, jest, etc.)
No .test.ts, .spec.ts, or __tests__/ files found in the entire repository
package.json scripts only define dev, build, and preview; no test command exists
detail.suggestion: Install vitest (already compatible with the Vite build): add vitest to devDependencies, add a 'test' script to package.json, and create at least a vitest.config.ts. Start with tests for github.ts since it contains the most critical parsing logic.

review::.::holistic::test_strategy::unte.summary: GitHub API response parsing and Link header extraction have zero test coverage

evidence: fetchJson performs HTTP calls with JSON parsing and error handling (lines 5-15) with no tests

fetchRepoData parses Link headers via regex (line 47: /page=(\d+)>; rel="last"/) to extract commit counts, with a fallback to 1 when no header exists but response is OK; this branching logic is untested
fetchAllRepoData deduplicates repo names and zips results back into a record; the index-based correlation between unique names and results is fragile without tests
detail.suggestion: Write unit tests for github.ts by mocking fetch: test fetchJson with success, HTTP error, and network error cases; test fetchRepoData with various Link header formats (present, absent, malformed); test fetchAllRepoData with duplicate repo names to verify deduplication and correct result mapping.

review::.::holistic::api_surface_coheren.summary: TerminalFrame interface defined locally in terminal.ts instead of centralized types module

evidence: terminal.ts defines TerminalFrame at lines 1-4, while artefacts.ts imports Artefact from $lib/types and projects.ts imports Project from $lib/types. The convention established by sibling data modules is to define data shapes in src/lib/types/index.ts.
detail.suggestion: Move the TerminalFrame interface to src/lib/types/index.ts alongside Project, Artefact, ProjectGitHubData, and SectionAccent. Update terminal.ts to import it from $lib/types.

security.summary: Security coverage complete for enabled detectors.
